mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.02% on 2007-08-01 represents late-cycle tightening
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% on 2007-08-01 near cycle lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts below trend signals weakening residential demand and construction activity, which reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1381.0 on 2007-07-01 showing early decline from peak
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between HOUST changes and subsequent SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in industrial production and payroll employment signals a supply-side shock or demand destruction that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% and PAYEMS declines > 50k in same month
evidence_refs: fact:Both series near cycle peaks in 2007-08
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 60% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in retail sales below the prior 3-month average signals weakening consumer demand and discretionary spending, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 377615.0 on 2007-08-01 near cycle peak
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPI) rises simultaneously, it signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through both higher discount rates and margin compression. The joint condition produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.02% and CPIAUCSL at 208.028 on 2007-08-01
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction effect is not significant (p > 0.05) in a multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment over a 2-month window signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income and consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. The mechanism is regime-conditional and most powerful during late-cycle expansions.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138037.0 on 2007-08-01 near cycle peak
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or the effect is not significantly stronger in late-cycle regimes (interaction p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in real GDP growth and housing starts signals broad-based demand destruction across both residential and overall economic activity. The interaction of these two demand indicators produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5% annualized and HOUST declines > 5% quarter-over-quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11523.8 and HOUST at 1381.0 on 2007-07-01
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 65% of jointly-triggered instances, or the joint effect is not significantly larger than the sum of individual effects (p > 0.05).
